message.content: ["Attention-based Multiple Instance Learning aggregators in medical imaging are prone to attention concentration, producing overconfident and unstable predictions.", "QG-MIL introduces a gated transformer aggregator with RMSNorm-based pre-normalization, per-head QK normalization, fine-grained attention output gating, and SwiGLU-style feed-forward modules to stabilize training and distribute attention uniformly without auxiliary losses.", "The best-performing QG-MIL variants outperform leading baselines on six benchmarks spanning whole-slide pathology and cell-level hematology with an average improvement of +6.1 mean macro F1 points.", "Ablation studies show that the QG-MIL design provides the most consistent cross-domain performance and tightest variance compared to selected baselines."]
message.reasoning_content:
We are asked to split the text into distinct facets: separate aspects, conditions, or sub-claims. Output a JSON array of 2-4 short strings, each self-contained, using the text's own terms, no invention, no overlap.

Let's parse the text:

1. "QG-MIL: A Gated Transformer Aggregator for Domain-Agnostic Multiple Instance Learning in Medical Imaging." - title, introduces method.
2. "Attention-based Multiple Instance Learning aggregators in medical imaging are prone to attention concentration, producing overconfident and unstable predictions." - Problem statement.
3. "We introduce QG-MIL, a gated transformer aggregator that addresses this through four synergistic architectural components: RMSNorm-based pre-normalization, per-head QK normalization, fine-grained attention output gating, and SwiGLU-style feed-forward modules." - Proposed method with components.
4. "Together, these design choices stabilize training and distribute attention more uniformly across instances without auxiliary losses, masking, or multi-stage regularization." - Effect of design.
5. "We evaluate QG-MIL across six benchmarks spanning whole-slide pathology and cell-level hematology, covering two fundamentally different MIL scales." - Evaluation scope.
6. "The best-performing QG-MIL variants outperform leading baselines on all six benchmarks, with an average improvement of +6.1 mean macro F1 points." - Performance result.
7. "Attention overlays and attention mass analysis confirm more distributed instance weighting." - Qualitative confirmation.
8. "Ablation studies show that while individual components can match the full model on specific datasets, the QG-MIL design provides the most consistent cross-domain performance and tightest variance when compared to selected baselines." - Ablation study findings.
9. "We release a configurable implementation to support reproducibility at: https://github.com/unica-visual-intelligence-lab/QG-MIL" - Reproducibility resource.

We need to extract 2-4 facets. They should be self-contained and use the text's own terms. Possible facets:

- The problem: Attention-based MIL aggregators suffer from attention concentration, leading to overconfident and unstable predictions.
- The proposed solution: QG-MIL uses RMSNorm-based pre-normalization, per-head QK normalization, fine-grained attention output gating, and SwiGLU-style feed-forward modules to stabilize training and distribute attention uniformly without auxiliary losses.
- Experimental results: QG-MIL outperforms baselines on six benchmarks spanning whole-slide pathology and cell-level hematology, with +6.1 mean macro F1 improvement, and shows more distributed attention.
- Ablation insight: Individual components can match full model on specific datasets, but the full QG-MIL design yields most consistent cross-domain performance and tightest variance.

But we must condense into 2-4 facets, no overlap, short strings. The instruction says: "each a self-contained facet in the text's own terms - no invention, no overlap." So we can pick key claims. Probably best to cover the main contributions: problem, method, results. Possibly add ablation finding. So 4 facets:

1. "Attention-based Multiple Instance Learning aggregators in medical imaging are prone to attention concentration, producing overconfident and unstable predictions."
2. "QG-MIL introduces a gated transformer aggregator with RMSNorm-based pre-normalization, per-head QK normalization, fine-grained attention output gating, and SwiGLU-style feed-forward modules."
3. "These design choices stabilize training and distribute attention more uniformly across instances without auxiliary losses, masking, or multi-stage regularization."
4. "The best-performing QG-MIL variants outperform leading baselines on six benchmarks spanning whole-slide pathology and cell-level hematology with an average improvement of +6.1 mean macro F1 points."

But facet 2 and 3 could be combined? The text separates them: facet 2 is introduction of components, facet 3 is their effect. Might be overlap. Could combine: "QG-MIL uses RMSNorm-based pre-normalization, per-head QK normalization, fine-grained attention output gating, and SwiGLU-style feed-forward modules to stabilize training and distribute attention uniformly without auxiliary losses." That combines method and effect. Then we'd have 3 facets: problem, method+effect, results. The ablation finding could be a fourth: "Ablation studies show that the full QG-MIL design provides the most consistent cross-domain performance and tightest variance compared to selected baselines." That's distinct. So four facets: problem, solution/design, evaluation results, ablation insight. All self-contained.

Make sure they don't overlap: Problem is distinct. Solution includes the components and their effect (stabilize training, distribute attention). Results are specific numbers and benchmarks. Ablation is about consistency and variance. They are separate aspects.

Check word limits: each string short. They are sentences from text.

Thus, output JSON array like:
["Attention-based Multiple Instance Learning aggregators in medical imaging are prone to attention concentration, producing overconfident and unstable predictions.", "QG-MIL introduces a gated transformer aggregator with RMSNorm-based pre-normalization, per-head QK normalization, fine-grained attention output gating, and SwiGLU-style feed-forward modules to stabilize training and distribute attention uniformly without auxiliary losses.", "The best-performing QG-MIL variants outperform leading baselines on six benchmarks spanning whole-slide pathology and cell-level hematology with an average improvement of +6.1 mean macro F1 points.", "Ablation studies show that the QG-MIL design provides the most consistent cross-domain performance and tightest variance compared to selected baselines."]

But note: The solution facet says "introduces ... to stabilize" – it's taken from text: "We introduce QG-MIL, a gated transformer aggregator ... Together, these design choices stabilize..." Combining them. I'll use that. Ensure no invention. Also the ablation facet is derived from "Ablation studies show that while individual components can match the full model on specific datasets, the QG-MIL design provides the most consistent cross-domain performance and tightest variance when compared to selected baselines." I'll keep it as in text.

All good. Output JSON.
